params.name: Khipu
params.description:
On Apr 11, 1974 AD, Harvard professor Urton successfully deciphered the Inca writing for the first time.
Khipu was a system of knots used by the Inca to record information. When studying Inca civilization, scientists were puzzled by the cotton strings left by the Inca. Why did the Inca have no form of writing? Finally, archaeologists from Harvard University deciphered that Khipu was a three-dimensional writing system. By using different knotting techniques and knot numbers, the Inca recorded …
